track: GBDT (LightGBM) ranker over symmetric features — cross-split (train seed123+seed7, eval seed42)
diagnosis: GBDT essentially RECONSTRUCTED LightGCN (corr_z 0.97). Given LightGCN + popularity + interaction features, the non-linear tree leaned overwhelmingly on lgcn_zwu (gain 94241, ~5x the next feature) and the popularity features (item_pop 7550, pop_zwu 6109) contributed little. This reproduces the boundary-analysis finding that item popularity residualized against LightGCN is chance. Both deltas (+0.0005 solo, +0.0006 blend) are within noise (0.0007) and far below MDE (0.00355) -> statistically tied, NOT a signal.
anti_trap_design_held: Trained AND gated on UNIFORM splits (public surrogate); CROSS-SPLIT (eval split's sampler never seen in training, unlike the failed logreg stacker); symmetric within-user-varying features only (no candidate-marginal review-side trap).
remaining_subvariant: TRUE multi-model collaboration (user explicitly requested): GBDT over {lightgcn, itemknn, ease, als} scores cross-split. Prior strongly negative (Stage2 alone public 0.74594; logreg stacker 0.75355) but worth completing the explicit request. Requires materializing Stage2 scores on seed123/seed7 folds.
